title: Ordered-Categorical CatBoost Specialist
summary: Your best runs still reduce high-cardinality race/driver context to frequency features.
rationale: Your best runs still reduce high-cardinality race/driver context to frequency features. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce prediction shift and target leakage on categorical-heavy tabular data, which matches this dataset’s many Driver/Race/Compound interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw categorical columns plus the current numeric tyre-window features; let CatBoost build category combinations such as Driver x Race, Driver x Compound, Race x Year, Compound x Stint. Validate with race-grouped folds and compare against the current frequency-encoded tree model, especially on unseen/rare drivers.
expected_effect: Small but real CV lift from better handling sparse category interactions and less leakage-prone target statistics.
risk: If folds are not grouped chronologically/by race, CatBoost can look better than it really is; raw IDs may also overfit rare entities without enough regularization.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
